62165024
Both sides in this thread are coping a bit.
NEET feels like freedom at first but for most people it turns into stagnation unless you're extremely self-directed. You lose structure, your sense of time gets weird, and eventually nothing feels that rewarding.
Wagie life sucks in a different way (routine, low autonomy, sometimes humiliation) but it gives you money, exposure to people, and at least some structure.
NEET + no direction = decay
Wagie + no progression = slow trap
NEET + discipline (learning, building, training) = rare but viable
Wagie + skill-building / saving = actually opens options over time
Most people in this thread are arguing about comfort vs discomfort, not outcomes.
Also: if you're the type who does nothing with your free time while working you're not suddenly going to become productive as a NEET. It usually gets worse, not better.
If you care about long-term quality of life, the question is: are you gaining leverage (skills, money, options) or just maintaining your current state?
Everything else is just preference and coping.

62165089
>>62158557
>wagie or a NEET
wagie because you can find yourself surround by negative people for a while

>>62165024

Jobs also don’t change your sense of time, all a job does is make you adhere to a schedule and some schedules are fucked up so it’s the same as NEETING. The wealth is concentrated at the top and most of the rich today inherited wealth they did NOT work for it AT ALL. Get that thru your fucking head. You are labor and no laborer ends up like Trump or Musk. Most labor ends up back broken and tossed aside later in life. 50% of all baby boomers are broke and destitute that’s proof enough waging isn’t the correct path

62165417
>>62158557
Waging is worse. The problem with waging is that most jobs are full time with a pitiful allotment of days off.
Like seriously, I get 4 weeks of personal leave a year. That amounts to a couple of 2 week holidays in a year, just fucking nothing. Its such a small amount of time I cant even stand to use it because im psychologically cucked knowing im going to be counting down those days before i have to get back to work again. And then when i get back to work, I will have fewer days left in the bank and the despair will set in further.
Being a neet was being a loser with no hope but at least you can put yourself in a state of mild contentment through trivial entertainment.
Being a wagie is a state of joylessness, boredom and stress. And then you're so time poor you cant enjoy anything in between so the money is irrelevant.
Waging is below me as a human being. I can work, I can feel fulfilled overcoming challenges and building towards things. But i can not, and will not hold up the merits of doing a constant stream of unending tasks 8 hours a day, 5 days a week. There is not a life for a conscious human.

62165497
>>62158557
Wagie. Dealing with normgroids sucks donkey penis.

62165528
The unfortunate truth is that being a NEET is basically the ultimate way of fucking yourself over, unless big inheritance is guaranteed. There's no way of putting into words how fucked a ~30 year old NEET is whose parents are getting up there in age and aren't wealthy. Even if you were in your early twenties, if you've been a NEET for even just a few years you have fucked yourself over massively.

It might not seem that way when you're sitting in your comfy room with electricity and food and bills getting paid for by someone else, but one day, that someone else will be gone. The lights will dim, that person won't be there to give you food or buy you things anymore, and there will be silence. And you'll be alone. And all those years you spent thinking "haha, those wagies are such cucks!" and "idc what happens to me I'll just rope doomer patrick bateman perfect girl edit slowed extended" won't have meant anything. Reality will hit you like the Malaysian Airlines flight 370 Boeing 777 hit the Indian Ocean doing a full speed nosedive. You will realize that you have lived over 1/4th of your life and you haven't begun a career and you have nothing to show for yourself, meanwhile young twenty something are working towards their masters degrees or already working salary jobs. And you'll realize something absolutely catastrophic: you CAN'T rope. You can't do it. There really is no escape, you are only now realizing that you can't bring yourself to do it. You don't have to die to go to hell, you just have to live your life as a NEET.

>>62165528
>tl:dr wageoid seething
Kys retard.

You run out of quality entertainment VERY quickly, and you'll start losing your mind playing MOBA trash or watching moeshit, or being a prominent member of a niche online community, and your days will be filled with nostalgia about enjoying things from years and years ago, now that's a fate worse than death.

And those are just the 23-25 year olds not to mention the ones in their later 20's with actual savings from their full time adult jobs. You on the otherhand live in a fairytale world where "everyone my age with money got it from mommy". Nope. Sorry bud. Some people are skilled and actually have good jobs.

I’m on my 15th year of doing commercial hvac. Were union, so the pay and benefits are good. We get to take our vans home and we have a company phone, all paid for. Dynamic work environment. Work alone most of the time. Micromanaging almost non existent. Flexible work schedule. Just name some of the pros.

Sure there are cons to it as well like anything else but some of those are non issues (you might sweat from time to time…). Guys who have been here as long or longer than me aren’t using walkers to get around. We’re all perfectly fit. There are tools for every job. The ‘broken body’ issue is a meme from people who never worked in the field or are hurting themselves. That goes for pipefitters, construction workers, etc. companies get away with shitty work environments because workers put up with it. I hate waging, period, but it’s the best thing I found so far. The sooner I can stop the better though

62165179
>>62159597
True words.

Anyone who's ever worked blue collar job understands that the real money is in operating a business (roofing, plumbing company, etc), not in the labor itself. Being self-employed blue collar or even employed blue collar will always keep you in the low income category or middle income at best.

It is just a psyop to further push down the cost of blue collar labor since unlike white collar work, it is hard to automate with AI. The value of physical, blue-collar work will be pushed down further as many people ditch white collar and try to move into blue-collar jobs due to AI automation, further pushing down the value of physical blue-collar labor.

Let the blue-collar goyim deal in the real world.

item.title: Why did CBDCs fail? — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why did CBDCs fail?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-28 20:08 UTC

OP

>Across Asia, the central bank digital currency (CBDC) revolution increasingly looks like a rebellion gone awry. Major Asian economies that once seemed certain to adopt a digital fiat currency are either struggling with low uptake (India), are reconsidering the merits of a CBDC (Japan) or have suspended it (Korea).
https://www.forbes.com/sites/digital-assets/2026/04/27/cbdcs-are-slowing-in-asia-but-china-is-sticking-to-its-e-cny-plans/

Early replies (first 3)

62163868
>>62163865
I still have no idea how CBDC are different to online banking with e-transfers

62163901
>>62163868
I don't think they know, either

62163923
>>62163868
Basically you take the vaccine and support trannies or they freeze your accounts

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62163936
>>62163868
CBDCs aren't stored in bank accounts. They're stored in digital wallets in the form of a file on a physical device. Transactions are done between two wallets directly (it's like sharing files - no middleman). Each CBDC (file) has a unique serial number and the central bank keeps track of all the serial numbers it has ever printed (so you can't counterfeit CBDCs).
The transaction itself doesn't require internet. So if you give your sister 10 bucks to blow you without either device being online, only the two of you know the transaction took place. But as soon as the device is connected to the internet, the Central Bank/Government (directly) knows that the transaction happened (from your wallet to your sister's wallet). It still doesn't know that you paid her to blow you.
It's basically a replacement for cash with enhanced traceability.
At least that's how I understand it (and this is how the e-yuan is being implemented IIRC).

62163954
>>62163865
India? Lmao half the fucking country doesn't have ELECTRICITY.

The whole concept is retarded in that one big fucking EMP will destroy all "money".

Inb4 impossible! Nigger we dropped two nukes. You think a big fucking radio wave isnt something possible?

62163968
>>62163954
India already has UPI. It's a similar concept to CBDCs, it just still uses banks as middlemen. You can read up on it.
Japan just signed a MoU with India to allow Indians to use UPI in Japan. Singapore, UAE, France, Mauritius, Nepal, Bhutan, Sri Lanka, and Qatar already allow it.

62163980
>>62163936
That's basically a cheque book, no need to reinvent the wheel. And it's not a replacement for cash by any means because you can pay/receive cash to/from anyone even if you don't trust them. But for cheques you must trust a person.

62163990
>>62163980
Cheques go through banks.

62164493
>>62163936

62164503
>>62163865

62164539
>>62163954

62164571
>>62163868

62166880
